metadata.notes: NL injury tracker. Community-maintained MLB injury database. Contributions welcome via PR.

injuries.details: Tore right ACL before 2021 All-Star break. Surgery and missed rest of 2021 and most of 2022.
Tore left ACL in Pittsburgh game. Surgery and rehab through 2024-2025. Two surgically repaired knees.
Not ready for Opening Day. First game back May 23 vs Padres — homered on first pitch after 362 days.
Late scratch from weightlifting. Next day hit leadoff HR. Cleared for All-Star Game.
Grade 1 strain vs Rockies at Coors Field. 10-day IL. Reinstated Aug 15.
Brief IL stint in late Aug after hamstring recovery.
7-game hitless stretch Sept. Moved from leadoff to 6th/7th by Snitker. Manager friction. Yankees trade interest.
Mystery stomach bug, no diagnosis after extensive testing. Lost ~20-25 lbs (175 to ~150) in two weeks. Missed Opening Day and Japan series.
Diminished strength, reduced bat speed, mechanical swing flaws. Battled shortstop transition. Career-low .258 AVG, .732 OPS. Declared 'season over' in early Aug.
Went through anger and sadness before accepting failure and reframing mindset in August.
Bouts of lower back discomfort managed day-to-day throughout season.
Career-worst regular season (.258 AVG) then .317 over final 47 games. 6 hits in Wild Card sweep vs Reds. Dodgers won 2025 World Series — Betts had clutch 2-run single Game 6.
